title: vitis flexuosa subsp. rufotomentosa
intro: Vitis flexuosa subsp. rufotomentosa, also known as the Himalayan grape, is a species of grapevine native to the Himalayan region. It is used in traditional medicine, particularly in Ayurveda, for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The plant is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and its extracts are also used in the production of herbal supplements and tinctures. Additionally, the leaves and fruits of the plant are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
